projectDescription: A native ROS 2 Python driver for the MKS/Oradar MS200 TOF LiDAR sensor that decodes serial packets and publishes standard LaserScan messages.
keyFeatures: Native Python implementation with no proprietary SDK dependencies
Automatic detection of MS200 serial frame header (0x54 0x2C)
Publishes standard sensor_msgs/LaserScan messages on /scan topic
Configurable serial port, baudrate, and TF frame ID via ROS 2 parameters
Compatible with Nav2 and SLAM Toolbox out of the box
Tested on Raspberry Pi 5 with ROS 2 Jazzy
publicApiSurface: MS200Node.read_serial_data() - timer callback to read and buffer serial data
MS200Node.publish_scan() - assemble and publish full 360-degree LaserScan message
